criteria_checked.note: Goal, D1-D4 decomposition, and out-of-scope list are unambiguous. Two wording nits (not blockers): (a) D1's ledger write-safety is 'modeled on the frozen tools out_refused', but out_refused REFUSES all in-tree paths (dmc-v0.5.0-run-metrics.sh:33), whereas the DEFAULT ledger .harness/metrics/ledger.jsonl IS in-tree — so the default must be special-cased ALLOW while only the --ledger override is out_refused-checked; spell this out. (b) Otherwise clear.

AC5 (ledger stays out of git) can pass VACUOUSLY: it only asserts `git status --short` shows no .harness/metrics entry after a post-suspend real-path probe. If the default-path write is silently refused/broken (e.g. out_refused applied to the in-tree default), nothing is written, git-status is clean, and AC5 PASSES for the wrong reason — masking a broken default code path that is the operator-facing path per D4/handbook. REQUIRED: AC5 must also assert the record command exits 0 AND `test -f .harness/metrics/ledger.jsonl` with >=1 line after the probe. Rest of the verification set is strong (AC1 append-only + byte-identical-on-refuse, AC2 leak grep -c 0, AC3 rollup determinism + skipped_malformed, AC4 CLI-vs-direct byte-compare).

Repo findings verified accurate: frozen --validate exits 1 on invalid (line 220); deterministic emit lines 88-102 with run_id/goal_type/efficiency_notes each on their own redacted line (parseable, newlines collapsed line 54); scope-guard exempts evidence+verification but NOT .harness/metrics (scope-guard.sh:159-163); .gitignore lacks .harness/metrics/ (D4 adds it); .harness/mode active; branch claude/dmc-fable-core at 3e0caf1.

All PATH refs correct. FACTUAL ERROR: the plan says '19 fields' (Current Repo Findings line 55) and AC1 requires 'all 19 schema fields' (line 162), but the frozen validator REQ array (dmc-v0.5.0-run-metrics.sh:55-57) and the schema JSON block (run-metrics.schema.md:9-30) both enumerate 20 required keys (confirmed by count). REQUIRED: change 19->20, or make AC1 count-agnostic ('all schema-required fields per the frozen validator REQ set'), so a faithful verifier does not trip on a 20-field row against a literal '19'.

Redaction-parity design is SOUND: free-form values (run_id/goal_type/efficiency_notes) are taken from the frozen REDACTED emit, numerics/enums from the validated input — the only secret-carriers pass the frozen redactor, numerics fail-close a secret-shaped string, enums are fixed sets. AC2 leak-probe genuinely falsifies a lazy 'read all fields from raw input' implementation. Append-only + no-gate-reads-ledger + no-auto-invocation are all internally consistent.

5 in-scope files with correct grants (new .py=create, 4 edits). Out-of-scope list explicit (no frozen-tool/hook/gate/lifecycle/installer/AGENTS-generator change). DEFAULT_PROTECTED (dmc-v0.2.6-gate-check-runner.sh:22-31) excludes bin/dmc and bin/lib — no DMC_GATE_PROTECTED override needed (plan correct); enforcement-class FLAG on bin/dmc + new lib is expected non-degrading, matching v1.0.5 precedent. Legacy 802/3/3 invariance CONFIRMED: legacy suite uses hardcoded NOFLAG_TOOLS/SELFTEST_TOOLS lists + PINNED_BASELINE {49,802,3,3} (dmc-legacy-selftest.py:45-118); mirror-check globs only dmc-v0.* and forbids stray dmc-v0.* files (lines 18,263) — the deliberate name dmc-metrics-recorder.py (no v0. prefix) avoids BOTH. Naming is a hard build constraint (already in plan).

Security lens (mandatory — enforcement-class landmark). PRIMARY leak vector (secret in free-form field) is closed by frozen-redactor parity + tested by AC2. Numeric fields validated so a secret cannot hide there. No env/.env/credential/network read by design. RECOMMENDED (not a blocker): the recorder is a NEW enforcement-class file that execs a subprocess + does file I/O — add a structural self-audit AC mirroring the frozen tool's AC6 (grep the operative source for os.environ/getenv/socket/urllib/curl/wget/--live), so the 'advisory, offline, no env/network read' posture is machine-PROVEN, not only asserted in the header comment. Bash-radius note: `bin/dmc metrics record --ledger <tmp>` (and the default-path form) contains NO write-idiom token, so classify_l1 returns NO-WRITE=ALLOW even when armed (dmc-bash-radius.py:398 only fires on `python -c`); the ledger write happens via Bash-subprocess Python open(), which neither bash-radius nor scope-guard (Edit/Write-only) adjudicates — the 'post-suspend' ordering is therefore a sound conservative choice to protect postbash-diff snapshot integrity, not a hard denial-avoidance requirement; the plan may state this rationale.

Fully additive: new file is deletable, the 4 edits are append/additive (.gitignore line, two doc sections, one MILESTONES entry). Legacy count + mirror-check invariant under the chosen name, so revert is clean (delete the new .py + revert bin/dmc + 3 doc/config appends). No migration, no state, no frozen-tool mutation.
